revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Haritsuna-jinja]][[ja:針綱神社]] {{Expand Japanese|針綱神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Haritsuna Shrine | native_name = 針綱神社 | image = Haiden of Haritsuna Shrine 20150124.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Haritsuna Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.387186|136.939914}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|https://www.haritsunajinja.com/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Haritsuna Shrine'''|針綱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11465292|ltq=Q11465292|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 59 ** P155: {{ill|Aguasa Shrine|lt=Aguasa Shrine|ja|阿具麻神社|qid=Q85884332|1=Aguma Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039503|ltq=Q135039503|qid=Q135039503|1=Ikutano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q91898213|ltq=Q91898213|qid=Q91898213|1=Inuyama}} * {{ill|Owari Province|lt=Owari Province|ar|أواري|ca|Província d'Owari|ckb|پارێزگای ئۆوواری|cs|Provincie Owari|de|Provinz Owari|en|Owari Province|es|Provincia de Owari|eu|Owari probintzia|fa|ولایت اوواری|fi|Owarin provinssi|fr|Province d'Owari|gl|Provincia de Owari|hak|Owari-koet|id|Provinsi Owari|it|Owari (provincia)|ja|尾張国|ko|오와리국|lt|Ovario žemė|nl|Owari (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Owari|pt|Província de Owari|ru|Овари (провинция)|simple|Owari Province|sk|Owari (provincia)|sr|Провинција Овари|tr|Owari (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Оварі|vi|Tỉnh Owari|wuu|尾张国|zh_min_nan|Owari-kok|zh_yue|尾張國|zh|尾張國|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Q1347719|ltq=Q1347719|qid=Q1347719|1=Niwa District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.387186|136.939914}} == official website (P856) == * https://www.haritsunajinja.com/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Haiden of Haritsuna Shrine 20150124.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181399 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == location (P276) == * {{ill|Inuyama Castle|lt=Inuyama Castle|az|İnuyama qəsri|ca|Castell d'Inuyama|cs|Inujama (hrad)|cy|Castell Inuyama|de|Burg Inuyama|en|Inuyama Castle|es|Castillo Inuyama|eu|Inuyama gaztelua|fa|قلعه اینویاما|fi|Inuyaman linna|fr|Château d'Inuyama|hu|Inujamai várkastély|hy|Ինույամա ամրոց|id|Kastel Inuyama|ja|犬山城|ka|ინუიამას ციხე-ქალაქი|ko|이누야마성|lt|Inujamos pilis|ml|ഇനുയാമ കാസിൽ|mr|इनुयामा किल्ला|pl|Zamek Inuyama|pt|Castelo de Inuyama|ru|Замок Инуяма|tr|Inuyama Kalesi|uk|Замок Інуяма|zh|犬山城|qid=Q1155484|1=Inuyama Castle}} == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 2419624 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9180005009259 == Commons category (P373) == * Haritsuna-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県犬山市大字犬山字北古券65-1', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県犬山市犬山', 'language': 'ja'} == significant event (P793) == * {{ill|Q11571216|ltq=Q11571216|qid=Q11571216|1=Inuyama Festival}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Torii of Haritsuna Shrine.JPG == museum-digital place ID (P9957) == * 24185 {{commons category|Category:Haritsuna-jinja}} [[JA:針綱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11648178}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記| date = November 2024}} {{神社 | 名称= Haritsuna Shrine | 画像= [[File:Haritsuna Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii | 所在地= {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|qid=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}Oaza Inuyama, Aza Kitakoken 65-1 | 位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|23|14.39|N|136|56|23.88|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Haritsuna Shrine}} | 祭神= Oji Harinane no Muraji no Mikoto | 社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建= Unknown | 本殿= {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} with copper-sheet roofing | 例祭= First Saturday and Sunday of April ({{ill|lt=Inuyama Festival|Q11571216|qid=Q11571216|1=Inuyama Festival}}) | 神事= | 別名= Hakusan-sha
Hakusan Daimyojin
Hakusan Haritsuna Daimyojin
Hakusan Myori Gongen | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Haritsuna Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[画像:Haritsuna-jinja Haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden]] [[画像:Haritsuna-jinja Soribashi.jpg|thumb|200px|Arched bridge]] [[画像:Haritsuna-jinja Tenjinsha.jpg|thumb|200px|Tenjin Shrine]] [[画像:Street view in front of torii of Haritsuna Shrine.JPG|thumb|200px|In front of Haritsuna Shrine]] '''Haritsuna Shrine''' (はりつなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama|Q503411|qid=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is identified with "Haritsuna Shrine" in Niwa District, Owari Province, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. It is said to grant blessings for safe childbirth and conception. This tradition comes from {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|qid=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|qid=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6), when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobuyasu|Q11608286|qid=Q11608286|1=Oda Nobuyasu}} dedicated a pair of guardian dogs carved by his own hand to pray for safe childbirth. Since then, praying on the Day of the Dog has been considered auspicious. Like {{ill|lt=Sanko Inari Shrine|Q11354440|qid=Q11354440|1=三光稲荷神社}}, it stands near the southern entrance route up to {{ill|lt=Inuyama Castle|Q1155484|qid=Q1155484|1=Inuyama Castle}}. The {{ill|lt=Inuyama Festival|Q11571216|qid=Q11571216|1=Inuyama Festival}} is this shrine's festival. === History === Until the Edo period it was called '''Hakusan-sha''' and was the guardian shrine of Inuyama Castle. Its foundation date is unknown. Its original site was near the castle keep, but in {{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|qid=Q6461|1=1537}} ({{ill|lt=Tenbun (era)|Q1192853|qid=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6) it was moved by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobuyasu|Q11608286|qid=Q11608286|1=Oda Nobuyasu}} to Hakusan-daira (the hill where {{ill|lt=Naritasan Nagoya Betsuin Daishoji|Q11495646|qid=Q11495646|1=Narita-san Temple}} now stands). In {{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|qid=Q6747|1=1606}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 11), castle lord {{ill|lt=Ogasawara Yoshitsugu|Q10860025|qid=Q10860025|1=Ogasawara Yoshitsugu}} moved it to Naguri-machi in the castle town. In {{ill|lt=1635|Q6879|qid=Q6879|1=1635}} ({{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 12), parade floats/processions from Shimohonmachi and Uoyamachi were presented for the shrine festival. In {{ill|lt=1641|Q6907|qid=Q6907|1=1641}} (Kanei 18), Shimohonmachi changed its horse tower to a wheeled float and dedicated mechanical dolls; this became the prototype of the Inuyama Festival. In {{ill|lt=1649|Q6945|qid=Q6945|1=1649}} ({{ill|lt=Keian|Q1146135|qid=Q1146135|1=Keian}} 2), castle lord {{ill|lt=Naruse Masatora|Q11495574|qid=Q11495574|1=Naruse Masatora}} encouraged wheeled floats and processions, making the festival even more active. In the mid-Edo period, scholars including {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} advanced the theory that this shrine corresponds to Niwa District's "Haritsuna Shrine" in the ''Engishiki Jinmyocho,'' and this became the prevailing view. Based on this, in {{ill|lt=1775|Q7700|qid=Q7700|1=1775}} (An'ei 4) it was renamed "Hakusan Haritsuna Daimyojin," and officially came to be called Haritsuna Shrine. However, this identification has dissenting views: {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} argued that the original Haritsuna Shrine was Ueno-mura Tenjin (present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tateno Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}). ''Tokusen Shinmeicho'' does not mention a specific identification. In the Meiji period it became a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}; in {{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 8) it became a {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. In {{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|qid=Q7812|1=1882}} (Meiji 15) it was moved to the current site and became a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oji Harinane no Muraji no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|1=UNKNOWN}} *:A descendant of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}; child of Otsunane-no-Mikoto; member of the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|qid=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}}. *{{ill|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Tamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyotama-hime}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Taketsutsukusa-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Taketane-no-Mikoto|Q11486691|qid=Q11486691|1=Takeinadane}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shirichirine-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oarata-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Onamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *{{ill|lt=Taketaori-no-Mikoto|Q97706292|qid=Q97706292|1=Taketahori no Mikoto}} *Otsunane-no-Mikoto === Precinct shrines === {{columns-list|colwidth=10em| *Shinmei Shrine *Ichi Shrine *Kotohira Shrine *Akiba Shrine *Yama Shrine *Sugikodama Shrine *Miki Shrine *Okuni Shrine *Inari Shrine *Oguri Shrine *Miketsu Shrine *Someho Shrine *Kaji Shrine *Atsuta Shrine *Tenjin Shrine *Sarutahiko Shrine *Atago Shrine *Hachiman Shrine *Mitake-no-miya Shrine }} === Transportation === *15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Inuyama Station|Q6059394|qid=Q6059394|1=Inuyama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Inuyama Line|Q6810411|qid=Q6810411|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} *10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Inuyama-Yuen Station|Q6059388|qid=Q6059388|1=Inuyama-Yūen Station}} on the Meitetsu Inuyama Line *Get off at "Inuyama Castle" stop on the {{ill|lt=Kakamigahara Fureai Bus|Q11412316|qid=Q11412316|1=Kakamigahara City Fureai Bus}} holiday circular route (runs weekends/holidays) === Other === {{commons category|Haritsuna-jinja}} *The guardian dogs carved by Oda Nobuyasu for safe-childbirth prayer are also said to have been modeled after {{ill|lt=Oda Hirochika|Q11608371|qid=Q11608371|1=Oda Hirochika}}. Oda Hirochika built {{ill|lt=Kinoshita Castle|Q11517925|qid=Q11517925|1=Kinoshita Castle}} in Inuyama in {{ill|lt=1469|Q6566|qid=Q6566|1=1469}} ({{ill|lt=Bunmei (Japan)|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei|1=Bunmei}} 1), and also built a fort near present Inuyama Castle, forming the prototype of Inuyama Castle. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Haritsuna Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Haritsuna Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Haritsuna Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:犬山市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Inuyama, Aichi]]
